name: Blueberries and Cream Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. For the crust: Combine cookie crumbs and butter. Press into the bottom and halfway up the sides of a greased 9-inch springform pan. Cover and refrigerate.
2. For the filling: Beat cream cheese in a large bowl for about 25 minutes, adding one package at a time. Add sugar and beat for an additional 5 minutes. Add eggs and egg yolks one at a time, beating two minutes after each addition. Beat in flour and vanilla. Stir in whipping cream. Preheat the oven to 500 degrees F (260 degrees C).
3. Pour filling into crust. Bake for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 200 degrees F (93 degrees C) and bake an additional hour. Turn off the oven and leave the cake inside, without opening the door, for one more hour. Cool completely on a wire rack.
4. Remove the sides of the pan. For the topping: Combine cornstarch and 3 tablespoons water in a small bowl; stir until smooth. In a saucepan, combine sugar and remaining 1 cup water. Cook over medium heat until the sugar dissolves. Stir constantly and add the cornstarch mixture. Continue cooking until the mixture boils and thickens. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature. Stir in blueberries. Spoon the topping over the cheesecake. Loosely cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours or overnight. Serve chilled.
